calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lou Aronian for and with Ronald Lim. Cecilia Lim (Beneficiary) in pro per.

Mr. Aronian addressed the litigation fees, presented argument, stated that Mr. Lim has used some of his own funds to keep the Estate moving, and that the beneficiaries agree to the fees being paid. In regard to the extraordinary fees, Mr. Aronian stated that there were two wills both of which were signed on the same date and witnessed by the same people, that there was a will contest filed by Mr. Lim's Sister, that they had to apply for Special Administrator until Mr. Lim was appointed, and that because of the will contest there was additional time needed to deal with some time consuming proceedings. At the conclusion of Mr. Aronian's argument, the matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Continued To Jan-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lou Aronian and Nils Rosenquest (Co-Counsel) for Ronald Lim (Administrator). Kent Hori and Brent Kato (Co-Counsel) for Genevieve Lim (Objector).

Mr. Aronian stated that the property had been sold, that the proceeds had been deposited in nine different bank accounts, that the taxes had been filed, that there was a will contest, and that there needed to be a cutoff time for creditor's to file claims. In addition, he noted that they had received an offer from the contestant but there was nothing further for him (them) to do at this point. The Court inquired as to the will contest. Mr. Hori stated that an offer was reduced to writing last week. Mr. Rosenquest stated the the estimated time for trial would be 10 days. Mr. Aronian asked the Court if the final account could be filed. The Court stated that he should wait to file the accounting, referred the matter to Pro Bono Mediation, and ordered that it be completed by January 6, 2009. Status on the mediation was set for January 13, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. The Court signed the order extending the special letters until further order of the court.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (30 Winter Place, San Francisco, Ca 94133)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lou Aronian with and for Ronald Lim (Special Administrator).

The sale of real property located at 30 Winter Place, San Francisco, was confirmed at $958,575.00, the Court granted the petition. No additional bond was required since counsel elected to place the funds in a blocked account. The matter was continued to September 10, 2008 at 9:00 a.m. for status on the blocked account. Attorney Lou Aronian to submit the order.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/Sajja Ravinantapricha. Probate Examiner: C. Nahnsen. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Continued To Nov-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kent Hori and Brent Kato for Genevieve Lim (Objector). Nils Rosenquest and Lou Aronian for Ronald Lim.

The parties stipulated to continuing the matter for 90 days. The matter was continued to November 4, 2008. The special letters will be extended to that date and Mr. Lim has the authority to open an account for the purpose of depositing proceeds from the sale of the real property.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Continued To Aug-05-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lou Aronian for Ronald Lim (Petitioner). Brent Kato for Genevieve Lim.

Mr. Aronian stated that there was one piece of real property in the estate, that there was no cash and that they were not anxious to sell the property. In addition, he stated that he wanted 4 months time to seek out buyers and that he believed they were close to a settlement. Mr. Kato stated that he did not object to the re-appoinment of Mr. Lim, that one of the heirs was deceased and requested a continuance to resolve the matter. The matter was continued to August 5, 2008. The Court signed the order appointing Mr. Lim as the Special Administrator. The letters will expire on August 5, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR#4609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-07-2008 Continued To Apr-28-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nils Rosenquest for Special Administrator, Ron Lim; Kent Hori for contestant, Genevieve Lim.

Mr. Rosenquest requested for a continuance to April 28, 2008. Mr. Rosenquest will go to the ex parte petition to re-appointing Mr. Lim as Special Administrator, which Mr. Hori agreed to. Matter is continued to April 28, 2008

Commissioner: Sue Kaplan; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: R. Hill; Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha/C. Herbert.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-04-2008 Continued To Apr-07-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nils Rosenquest and Lou Aronian for Ronald Lim. Brent Kato with Kent Hori for Genevieve Lim (Contestant).

Mr. Rosenquest stated that he was an associate in to deal with the will contest, that the other heirs and benificiaries needed to be served, and that it was premature to sell the real porperty. Mr. Aronian stated that the property was valued at $600,000.00 to $800,000.00, and that the administrators authority ran out as of this morning. Mr. Kato stated there was no reason for the sale to be compelled, that Betty Lim, one of the benificiaries had passed, and that no on had been appointed to handle that estate. All conusel agreed to go to Pro Bono Mediation, but not all parties were present. The Court extended the letters with the same limited terms to April 7, 2008. The Court granted Mr. Rosenquest request to use estate funds to pay taxes and maintain the property. The matter was continued to April 7, 2008 for status and to get Betty Lim's estate to the table. Mr. Hori has 15 days to respond to the complaint. Mr. Aronian will prepare the order extending the letters.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-03-2007 Continued To Feb-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lou Aronian for and with Ronald Lim. Brent Kato and Kent Hori for Genevieve Lim.

Mr. Aronian stated that the four page will is submitted for probate. Mr. Kato stated that the contest applies to both wills. Mr. Aronian requested his client be appointed as a special administrator and expressed concern about his client incurring cost for answering the will contest. Mr. Kato stated that he agreed that Mr. Lim should be appointed only for the purpose of managing the porperty, and not for use of estate funds for defense of the will contest. The Court is appionting Mr. Lim as the Special Administrator with limited powers to manage the property, and list the property for sale subject to court confirmation, (signing the order for the same in open court). No bond is required. The language of testate or intestate was struck from the Order. The letters expire on February 4, 2008 and the matter was also continued to that date. The Court is not authorizing funds in the estate or proceeds from the sale to be used for the will contest defense.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Rich Lee.
